title: [CI:DOCS] socket_activation.md: remove conmon from socket inheritance
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *eriksjolund* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign umohnani8 for approval. For more information see [the Kubernetes Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I'm pretty sure that the `conmon` process should be removed from the graph that describes how the socket is inherited from process to process. (The socket does not seem to be inherited via conmon).

Here is a link to the edited graph
https://github.com/eriksjolund/podman/blob/docs_remove_conmon_from_socket_inheritance/docs/tutorials/socket_activation.md#socket-activation-of-containers

What do you think?
Conmon is executing the oci runtime to create the container. So the order is systemd->podman->conmon->oci runtime-> container process. I think the current version is correct.

If conmon is the parent of crun, shouldn't the text output

```
podman --runtime /home/test/crun.bash run -q docker.io/library/alpine /bin/true 
```

in the following example have been different?

```
$ emacs ~/crun.bash
$ cat ~/crun.bash
#!/bin/bash
echo started crun.bash
cat /proc/$PPID/cmdline | tr '\0' ' '
echo
exec crun "$@"
$ chmod 755 ~/crun.bash
$ podman --runtime ~/crun.bash run -q docker.io/library/alpine /bin/true
started crun.bash
podman --runtime /home/test/crun.bash run -q docker.io/library/alpine /bin/true 
$ 
```

You need to write the PPID command to a file not stdout. conmon fork/exec's and dups the std streams to /dev/null.

The create call is always made by conmon:
```
/usr/bin/conmon --api-version 1 -c 32384d4182ae93cdb1405ed0a4ce500229456b3c65a3ebf598e283f16633cc24 -u 32384d4182ae93cdb1405ed0a4ce500229456b3c65a3ebf598e283f16633cc24 -r /home/pholzing/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/crun.bash -b /home/pholzing/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/32384d4182ae93cdb1405ed0a4ce500229456b3c65a3ebf598e283f16633cc24/userdata -p /run/user/1000/containers/overlay-containers/32384d4182ae93cdb1405ed0a4ce500229456b3c65a3ebf598e283f16633cc24/userdata/pidfile -n friendly_babbage --exit-dir /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/exits --full-attach -s -l journald --log-level warning --conmon-pidfile /run/user/1000/containers/overlay-containers/32384d4182ae93cdb1405ed0a4ce500229456b3c65a3ebf598e283f16633cc24/userdata/conmon.pid --exit-command /usr/bin/podman --exit-command-arg --root --exit-command-arg /home/pholzing/.local/share/containers/storage --exit-command-arg --runroot --exit-command-arg /run/user/1000/containers --exit-command-arg --log-level --exit-command-arg warning --exit-command-arg --cgroup-manager --exit-command-arg systemd --exit-command-arg --tmpdir --exit-command-arg /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp --exit-command-arg --network-config-dir --exit-command-arg  --exit-command-arg --network-backend --exit-command-arg netavark --exit-command-arg --volumepath --exit-command-arg /home/pholzing/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes --exit-command-arg --db-backend --exit-command-arg boltdb --exit-command-arg --transient-store=false --exit-command-arg --runtime --exit-command-arg /home/pholzing/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/crun.bash --exit-command-arg --storage-driver --exit-command-arg overlay --exit-command-arg --events-backend --exit-command-arg journald --exit-command-arg container --exit-command-arg cleanup --exit-command-arg 32384d4182ae93cdb1405ed0a4ce500229456b3c65a3ebf598e283f16633cc24 
podman --runtime /home/pholzing/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/crun.bash run -q docker.io/library/alpine /bin/true 
/usr/bin/podman --root /home/pholzing/.local/share/containers/storage --runroot /run/user/1000/containers --log-level warning --cgroup-manager systemd --tmpdir /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp --network-config-dir  --network-backend netavark --volumepath /home/pholzing/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes --db-backend boltdb --transient-store=false --runtime /home/pholzing/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/crun.bash --storage-driver overlay --events-backend journald container cleanup 32384d4182ae93cdb1405ed0a4ce500229456b3c65a3ebf598e283f16633cc24 
```
